topic: EXP-268 integration: three additive robustness footnotes (P1 projection convention, P2 altitude convention, P3 NN-tour replacement / probability framing) addressing the three moderate holes in EXP-260 c1 review.
our_argument_summary: §4.2.7a quantifies the 4.2.7 trilemma’s “Borrowed” branch: projecting the dome’s nine published eclipse stations onto the disc using AE-from-N and computing min-distance to the projected NASA umbra central line for Aug 12 2026 yields 0/9 within the 42 km penumbra radius and 0/9 within the 85 km penumbra band. Closest station (Ebro) is 444 km from the path, farthest (Canary) 4,084 km. Under the strict constant-r orbital reading, max one station can be in the band. EXP-268 adds three robustness paragraphs: (P1) the kill survives the alternative R_disc/180 AE convention common in flat-earth cartography, with all 9 stations 222–2,042 km from the path under that reading; (P2) the kill strengthens under the dome’s alternative “physical” Sun altitude (h_Sun ≤ 4,200 km) when h_Moon is scaled proportionally; (P3) replaces the fragile “NN tour 16,439 km” claim with a start-point-robust “≥12,000 km from any starting point” bound and an order-of-magnitude probability framing (10^-27 under IID-uniform null).

drift_check.notes: Headline 0/9 result and per-station distances (444 – 4,084 km, multiples 10.6×–97.2× R_p) are unchanged from the c1-reviewed EXP-260. EXP-268 only adds three additive paragraphs; it does not restructure the section. The SVG asset (docs/assets/eclipse-stations-vs-penumbra-band.svg, 7,319 bytes) is committed and present, closing the only critical hole from c1. The c1 minor holes H5 (R_p ∝ h_Moon scaling caveat), H6 (SNK vs INTERMAGNET GUI footnote), and H7 (NASA umbra sample list link) are not addressed by EXP-268 and remain open as low-priority polish.

code_analysis_tags_note: Not applicable: SEC-4.2.7a is a derived geometric argument over public NASA umbra data and the dome’s published h_Sun / h_Moon / station list. monitoring/relabels_standard/post_hoc/derives_from_dome are WIN-level tags and do not transfer to this section. The section IS, however, an empirical kill of the dome’s eclipse-prediction-set (which would be tagged ‘derives_from_dome:false / relabels_standard:false’ if it were a single WIN — the dome publishes per-station coverages but the geometric path that places stations in the band is borrowed from globe physics, never derived from disc parameters).

holes_found.description: P1 robustness paragraph contains a self-contradicting clause: “The Moon altitude required to inflate R_p to even the closest min-distance under that convention is about 123,000 km — still ~49× the dome’s published 2,534 km.” The 123,000 km / 49× figure corresponds to inflating R_p to Canary’s min-distance of 2,042 km — i.e., the FURTHEST station, not the closest. To reach the closest station (Ebro at 222 km under the stricter convention) you only need R_p = 222 km, h_Moon = 2534 × 222/42 ≈ 13,400 km, ratio ~5.3× the published value. The math_verification block in EXP-268 confirms 123,191 km is computed from 2042/42 (Canary, the farthest), so the prose is wrong, not the calculation. A skeptical defender will catch this inversion and use it to argue the entire P1 paragraph is sloppy. The kill is unaffected (under either reading, the required Moon altitude is many multiples of the published value), but the wording undermines confidence in the rest.
holes_found.recommendation: Replace “to inflate R_p to even the closest min-distance under that convention” with “to inflate R_p enough to bring even the most distant station (Canary, 2,042 km) into the band”. Or: “to inflate R_p enough to reach all nine stations”. Numbers (123,000 km, ~49×) stay the same.

holes_found.description: P2 robustness paragraph asserts: “scaling h_Moon proportionally to ~1,860 km” when h_Sun is set to the dome’s alternative physical 4,200 km limit. The dome page does NOT explicitly publish a scaled physical h_Moon; it publishes h_Moon = 2,534 km (period). The patch is making a charity choice that proportional refraction-scaling applies to both h_Sun and h_Moon. That is defensible (“if observed altitudes are refraction-bent, both are bent”) but it is the patch’s assumption, not a dome-published fact. Without an explicit one-sentence justification, a defender can claim the patch invented the 1,860 km figure and that under fixed h_Moon = 2,534 km plus h_Sun = 4,200 km the geometry breaks down completely (h_Sun — h_Moon = 1,666 km, formula domain shifts). That alternative reading would not save the dome — it just produces a different incoherence — but it is not what the patch addresses.
holes_found.recommendation: Add half a sentence to P2: “…implying both observed altitudes are refraction-bent readings of the smaller physical altitudes; scaling h_Moon proportionally to ~1,860 km…” — makes the assumption explicit. No numbers change.

holes_found.description: P3 probability framing uses “drawing nine station positions uniformly at random across the 1.26 billion km² disc surface” as the null model and arrives at 10^-27. Magnetic observatories are NOT IID-uniform on a disc; they cluster in inhabited mid-latitude continents (especially Europe — INTERMAGNET’s densest region). A defender can argue “of course your nine stations cluster near a European eclipse path; pick a clustered null and your 10^-27 vanishes.” Independent verification: under a tighter ‘IID-uniform within the European swath (lat 25–65°N, lon ±25°)’ null, P(9 in arc band) is on the order of 10^-16 – 10^-21. Still vanishingly small — the kill survives — but the patch should pre-empt this critique with a sentence rather than letting the IID-uniform claim stand alone.
holes_found.recommendation: Add one sentence to P3 after the 10^-27 figure: “Even under a stricter null restricting candidate stations to the European mid-latitude swath where INTERMAGNET observatories actually live, the same probability is 10^-16 to 10^-21 — still a vanishing chance match.” Optional: include the calculation in a footnote.

holes_found.description: P3 says “the shortest greedy nearest-neighbour route runs about 12,400 km, longer routes run to 16,400 km.” Independent recomputation (NN tour from each of the nine stations as start): shortest is Canary-start at 12,396 km (matches), but longest is Eskdalemuir-start at 18,811 km, NOT 16,400 km. The 16,438 km figure was the Ebro-start NN length — the same number that anchored the original (now-replaced) section text. The patch retained this value as the upper end without recomputing the actual maximum across starting points. The error UNDERSTATES the kill (a longer worst-case NN tour vs the ~4,500 km arc is more dramatic, not less), so it doesn’t hurt the argument, but it is an arithmetic error a defender can flag as ‘patch authors did not redo the calculation they were patching.’
holes_found.recommendation: Either drop the “16,400 km” upper end (and just state “the shortest greedy NN route is ~12,400 km from the most favorable start”), or update to “longer routes run to ~18,800 km depending on start point.”

holes_found.description: P3 claims tours “span at least 12,000 km on the disc from any starting point.” Independent verification confirms this is true for the greedy NN heuristic (minimum is Canary-start at 12,396 km). It is NOT a proven lower bound on optimal-TSP tour length — the optimal Hamiltonian path could in principle be shorter than NN. The math_verification block in EXP-268 hand-waves an MST argument (“any Hamiltonian tour traverses every MST edge at least once” — not literally true; a Hamiltonian path is bounded below by MST weight, but not in the way described). The argument doesn’t depend on the exact 12,000 km — even an optimal tour of these nine points across Europe is comfortably > 4,500 km — but a defender attacking the rigor of the bound has a small foothold.
holes_found.recommendation: Reframe as “The greedy nearest-neighbour heuristic gives 12,400–18,800 km depending on start. The true optimal Hamiltonian tour is bounded below by the minimum spanning tree (≈7,000 km for these stations) — still 1.5× the realistic 4,500 km arc.” Or simpler: keep the area-coverage / probability argument as load-bearing and demote the NN bound to a single supporting clause.

holes_found.description: EXP-268 addresses the three c1 moderate holes (H1 projection, H2 altitude, H3 NN-fragility) but does not touch the three c1 minors: H5 (R_p ∝ h_Moon linear-scaling caveat noted in original 246,000 km calculation), H6 (Canary station code SNK vs INTERMAGNET’s GUI), H7 (NASA umbra-path sample list not directly linked / reproducible). These were minor in c1 and remain minor; flagging here only so they aren’t lost in the patch-cycle bookkeeping.
holes_found.recommendation: Backlog. Roll into the next §4.2.7a touch — a single short footnote could close all three.

holes_found.description: P1 notes that under the stricter convention the closest pair (Coimbra–San Pablo) Δr halves to 75 km — “just inside the 85-km band, which would shift the strict constant-r reading from ‘max 1 station in band’ to ‘max 2.’” But independent recomputation shows a SECOND pair also drops below 85 km under that convention: Ebro–Coimbra Δr = 165/2 = 82.5 km < 85 km. So under the stricter convention, the constant-r band could in principle contain Ebro+Coimbra (Δr 82) OR Coimbra+San Pablo (Δr 75) — but NOT all three at once (Ebro–San Pablo Δr = 157 km > 85 km). “Max 2 in band” remains correct. However, the patch text presents “the closest-pair Δr” singular, which underplays this second near-miss and gives a defender a small angle of attack (“you cherry-picked the closest pair”). The conclusion “max 2” is still right; the reasoning could be tightened.
holes_found.recommendation: Update to: “the two closest pairs (Coimbra–San Pablo at 75 km, Ebro–Coimbra at 82 km) both halve into the band, allowing at most two of the three stations in the constant-r reading; Ebro–San Pablo still spans 157 km > 85 km, so all three cannot share the band.”

stronger_arguments.description: Generalize the IID-uniform probability to multiple plausible nulls in a single short paragraph. The reader sees “10^-27 under IID-uniform → 10^-21 under European-swath → 10^-9 even under ‘IID over the world’s ~150 active magnetic observatories’” as a single sequence and the kill is robust to null choice. Currently the patch only states the most-charitable-to-the-dome null; a defender can score against that without the reader seeing the answer.
stronger_arguments.recommendation: Optional polish; would strengthen P3 against the Bayesian-priors / null-model attack vector.

stronger_arguments.description: Cross-eclipse generalization (carry-over from c1 S1, still applicable). The dome’s nine-station array reportedly recurs across multiple predicted eclipses. If 0/9 in band holds under dome geometry for Aug 12 2026 (this section), it almost certainly holds for the other dome-predicted eclipses too — the geometry inputs (h_Sun, h_Moon, R_p) are eclipse-independent. A closing paragraph showing the same 9-station selection appears in the dome’s predictions for [eclipse 2], [eclipse 3] would convert this from a one-shot kill into a systematic-borrowing claim.
stronger_arguments.recommendation: Mark for next §4.2.7a expansion. Not blocking for the current EXP-268 integration.

kernel_of_truth.description: The EXP-268 patches are mostly very good. P1 and P2 do exactly what robustness paragraphs are supposed to do: anticipate the convention-defender, run the same calculation under their convention, and show the kill survives. P3’s replacement of the 16,439 km NN claim with a start-point-robust lower bound + probability framing is a clean strengthening. The structural kill (0/9 stations in the dome-geometry penumbra band, factor of 49–97 separation in required Moon altitude) is now hardened against the three highest-leverage defensive openings.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: N/A — this patch is on the offensive side (our review). The patches do not save any dome claim; they harden our kill of the dome’s 9-station list. The wording bug in P1 (‘closest min-distance’ vs the cited 49× number) is reparable in one line and does not threaten the kill itself.

recommended_action_detail: Two patches needed: (1) Fix P1 wording: change ‘to inflate R_p to even the closest min-distance’ to ‘to inflate R_p enough to reach even the most distant station (Canary, 2,042 km)’ — the cited 123,000 km / 49× figure becomes consistent with the prose. (2) Fix P3 upper-end NN tour from ‘16,400 km’ to either drop the upper end or update to ‘~18,800 km’ (longest is Eskdalemuir-start). Optionally: (3) one-sentence preempt of the IID-uniform null critique in P3, (4) one-sentence justification for proportional h_Moon scaling in P2, (5) tighten P1’s ‘closest pair’ to ‘two closest pairs’. Patches 1–2 are mandatory; 3–5 are polish.

text_fingerprint.tldr_first_sentence: Section 4.2.7 closes with a trilemma; this addendum quantifies its “Borrowed” branch.

deeper_analysis:
The c1 review of EXP-260 found one critical (missing SVG — since fixed), three moderates (H1–H3), and three minors (H5–H7). EXP-268’s three patches address H1, H2, H3 directly and additively. This is the cleanest possible response to a review: targeted patches that don’t restructure the section, don’t change headline numbers, and make the kill more robust rather than tweaking it.

What went well: (i) The math reproduces. Independent recomputation gives Ebro 222 km, Canary 2,042 km, closest-pair Δr 75 km under the stricter convention — all numbers in P1 match exactly. h_Moon scaled to ~1,856 km (rounded to 1,860) under the proportional-altitude reading is correct, and R_p → 31 km, band → 62 km are consistent. (ii) P3’s shift from a single fragile NN claim to two robust frames (≥1km bound + probability) substantively hardens the time-distribution rebuttal. (iii) The 10^-27 figure (0.001^9) is mathematically correct under its stated null.

What needs fixing: (i) P1’s prose says ‘closest min-distance’ but the cited 123,000 km / 49× number corresponds to the FURTHEST station’s min-distance (Canary 2,042 km → 49× R_p → 49× h_Moon). This is a wording inversion, not a math error — the math_verification block in EXP-268 explicitly computes 2534×2042/42=123,191. The fix is one-line. (ii) P3 says ‘longer routes run to 16,400 km’ but the actual longest NN tour (Eskdalemuir-start) is 18,811 km. The 16,438 km appears to be carried over from the Ebro-start figure that anchored the OLD section text (which was specifically what P3 was replacing). One-line fix or drop the upper end. (iii) The IID-uniform null model in P3 is the most charitable to the dome (largest sample space → smallest probability). A defender will challenge it. The kill survives a tighter European-swath null (≈10^-16 to 10^-21), but the patch should briefly say so.

What the curmudgeon role demands: even though this patch package is solidly executed and the kill survives all charity choices, the wording bug in P1 is exactly the kind of defender-discoverable inversion that would let a hostile reader claim ‘they don’t even understand which station is closest.’ That has to be fixed before the section sees broad attention. The other minors are nice-to-have polish.

TLDR check: passes. The TLDR’s ‘Even the closest station (Ebro) sits 444 km from the projected umbra path, ten times the penumbra radius’ is accurate (444 km / 42 km = 10.6×) and consistent with the body. ‘The dome’s own parameters require seven to nine of its nine named stations to record exactly zero magnetic eclipse signal’ is supported by the constant-r reading’s ‘max 1’ result.

Verdict: section is publishable in current form; the moderate-severity P1 wording bug should be patched in the next decider cycle. No verdict change for the section’s underlying claim. The C1→C2 progression is genuine net improvement.

citations_verified: raw-text/03-predictions.txt link target (internal repo path, 03-predictions.txt is present in raw-text/ and contains the 9-station array)
Aug 12 2026 NASA umbra path coordinates (greatest eclipse 65.0 N, 25.2 W, 17:46:13 UT) match NASA SE catalog
h_Sun=5,733 km, h_Moon=2,534 km, R_disc=20,015 km — dome-published parameters (CLAUDE.md confirms)
Standard AE-from-N projection r=(90-φ)·R_disc/90 — textbook flat-earth cartography
citations_unchecked: Specific NASA Besselian element values for Aug 12 2026 (assumed correct per NASA SE; not directly recomputed)

h_Sun_physical ≤ 4,200 km firmament-limit attribution to the dome’s eclipse page (referenced as ‘the dome’s eclipse page also publishes’ — not directly cited; if the dome page does NOT publish this, P2’s premise fails)

user_preference_consideration: Considered the other side at length. The strongest dome-defender attack on this section is now the IID-uniform null model in P3 (the patch makes the strongest claim where the assumption is most contestable). Even there, tighter nulls give 10^-9 to 10^-21 — the kill survives. The next strongest dome-side angle is the P1 wording inversion — a hostile reader could use ‘they confused closest with farthest’ to discredit the whole P1 paragraph. Both are addressed in the recommendations above. Steel-manning the dome: the most charitable reading of the dome’s 9-station list is that the dome AUTHOR did not derive the list from disc geometry, but used the standard-physics path to choose stations and then attached his coverage labels (which match standard physics, per the kernel-of-truth in c1). Under that reading, the dome’s position is ‘the page is descriptive, not derivational’ — which is exactly the trilemma’s ‘Borrowed’ branch this section quantifies. The dome cannot escape by retreating to descriptive-only without conceding the borrowing.
summary_for_decider: EXP-268 integration is good but contains one moderate wording bug (P1 ‘closest min-distance’ vs cited 49×/123,000 km figure that actually corresponds to FURTHEST station) and one minor arithmetic error (P3 upper-end NN tour stated as 16,400 km, actual is 18,800 km). Recommended action: minor_edit — two one-line fixes. The structural kill (0/9 in band, robust to convention/altitude/null choices) is intact and stronger than before EXP-268. C1 minors H5/H6/H7 remain open; backlog. SVG asset (the only c1 critical) is committed and present. Suggest queueing a small follow-up EXP for the two wording/arithmetic fixes plus the IID-uniform null pre-empt.
